_README: Custom/precision parts that still need to be proven out (designed in cad/, not yet built+verified on the car). Drives the PARTS screen. status: unproven=critical unknown, designed=CAD done awaiting measure/fab/fit, proven=built & verified.

legend.unproven: Critical unknown — not yet measured / built / tested
legend.designed: CAD done — awaits measure, fab & fit

parts.name: Adapter plate + coupler
parts.what: Bolts the EM57 motor face to the 944 torque-tube bellhousing and locates both shafts concentric.
parts.risk: The single precision part of the build. The coupler/hub is usually SOURCED (EV Coupler Connection), not machined — the EM57 output is an integrated reduction-gear interface, not a plain shaft.
parts.prove: Measure EM57 face + bellhousing → fill the .scad → machine → dry-fit → shaft runout < 0.1 mm → driveline rotates true by hand.

parts.name: Motor mounts
parts.what: Steel brackets tying the EM57 to the 944 factory crossmember hardpoints — locate the motor and react its torque.
parts.risk: Must bolt to crossmember hardpoints (never sheetmetal), be triangulated / fatigue-safe, and isolate NVH with bushings.
parts.prove: Measure EM57 bosses + crossmember points → weld steel → bolt up → verify height/alignment for the adapter → load-drive, inspect for cracks / whine.

parts.name: Battery enclosure — MAIN (8 modules)
parts.what: Welded 3 mm aluminum box for 8 modules in the fuel-tank bay, flanged to floor hardpoints.
parts.risk: The 26" (685 mm) Tesla module is the tight dimension — may need on-edge or 2-layer. Crash-load mounts must hit frame, not sheetmetal.
parts.prove: Put measured bay L×W×H into the .scad (auto fit-check) → fab → modules secured & sealed → bolt to hardpoints → megger isolation pass.

parts.name: Battery enclosure — FRONT (6 modules)
parts.what: Same design, smaller — 6 modules in the engine bay for front weight and the 50/50 balance.
parts.risk: Shares the fit + crash-mount constraints, and competes for space up front with the motor, inverter and charger.
parts.prove: Same as MAIN: bay fit-check → fab → secure & seal → hardpoint mount → isolation pass.

parts.name: Skid plate
parts.what: Sacrificial 3–5 mm steel plate under the low main box; takes road strikes and sets ground clearance.
parts.risk: Sets the practical breakover/clearance; must deflect (turned-up lip), not catch, and be replaceable.
parts.prove: Size to the main-box footprint + margin → mount to frame rails → clearance / breakover check → strike test, confirm the pack is untouched.

parts.name: Dash panel + gauge faces
parts.what: Custom panel/overlays repurposing the 944 gauges for EV values (MPH, motor RPM, SOC, HV).
parts.risk: Depends on the CAN → gauge adapter driving the stock needles correctly (ADR-0012).
parts.prove: Pick overlay vs new panel → print faces → drive gauges from the CAN adapter → confirm each reads the right EV value across its range.

parts.name: CAN → gauge adapter
parts.what: Small board translating ZombieVerter / openinverter CAN frames into the analog signals the stock 944 cluster needs (ADR-0012).
parts.risk: Custom electronics — needle calibration and signal mapping are unproven, and there's no CAD model yet.
parts.prove: Bench-map each CAN signal → drive a single gauge → calibrate the needle sweep → verify all four gauges live on the bus.
